Attribute.value: A prosecutor said the object that crashed near Poland’s eastern border was likely a large military drone, with evidence suggesting explosive damage and possible foreign origin. Investigators continue examining debris and flight path, with early indications the drone may have come from the direction of Belarus, increasing security concerns.

Object.name: report
Object.description: Report object to describe a report along with its metadata.

Attribute.value: Polish authorities found wreckage of what appears to be a Russian Shahed-136 drone about 100 km inside Poland near Osiny, but initially described it as an “unidentified object” with no recorded airspace violation. The incident echoes past cases where initial denial gave way to evidence of incursions by Russian drones into NATO airspace
